business_use: scan material, lot, serial, equipment, location, pallet, shipment, receipt or worker context as an intent that must be revalidated
used_by_root: CYCLE_COUNT (Cycle Count); canonical_target=CYCLE_COUNT; domain=D-05 Inventory & Logistics
used_by_workflow: CYCLE_COUNT scan-first identity/lot/serial/location confirmation; workflow command binding pending B/C10
online_behavior: online scan validates canonical id, tenant/site, workflow state, record version, qualification, hold/release status and duplicate-scan policy before any command is accepted
offline_capture_behavior: capture raw scanned value + parsed candidate + operator/device/site + local sequence + event time; do not commit authority while offline unless explicitly whitelisted by root owner
replay_policy: replay in local FIFO grouped by root record and scan session; dedup by scan fingerprint + idempotency key + device local sequence; revalidate latest server authority before applying
conflict_policy: duplicate scan deduped; unknown alias quarantined; changed lot/serial/release/hold/location status blocks apply and opens reconciliation task
audit_or_evidence_effect: append-only audit event with device_id, operator/system identity, tenant/site, local_sequence, event_time, receive_time, idempotency key, correlation/causation id; evidence link required when root policy says evidence; root_status=target_canonical
safety_boundary: scan confirms identity only; it never bypasses material hold, release, training, safety, quality or workflow guard
security_requirement: device registration, authenticated operator, tenant/site lock, offline queue integrity, tamper-evident local sequence, barcode/RFID parser allow-list, privacy redaction
frontend_indicator: scan pending badge, unresolved alias banner, duplicate-scan marker, conflict queue, disabled apply reason
api_event_binding: planning-only C06 binding: cycle_count.offline_scan_intent.captured | cycle_count.offline_replay.applied | cycle_count.offline_replay.conflict_detected; no AsyncAPI/OpenAPI/schema generated
stop_rule: block if canonical id cannot be resolved, root authority changed, hold/release status changed, operator not qualified, device untrusted, or scan would move material/equipment without approved workflow
